examples.description: runner with right knee pain — antalgic gait, special test + ROM degrees + 0-5 strength, MRI, stated dx + plan

transcript.text: Right knee pain when I run, started about a month ago.
Watching you walk, there's an antalgic gait favouring the right — you're offloading the right leg.
McMurray is positive on the right. Knee flexion to 120 degrees. Quadriceps strength five out of five.
The MRI of the right knee shows a medial meniscus tear.
My working diagnosis is a right medial meniscus tear.
Plan is physiotherapy, and we'll consider an orthopedic referral if there's no improvement in six weeks.

claims.text: Patient reports right knee pain with running.
claims.source_quote: Right knee pain when I run

claims.text: Patient reports onset approximately one month ago, associated with running.
Physician noted an antalgic gait favouring the right, with offloading of the right leg.
